quick_skim.title: Advances in Scientific Machine Learning for Coupled Fluid Flow and Transport
quick_skim.abstract: This chapter reviews recent advances in Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) for modeling coupled fluid flow and transport phenomena governed by the incompressible Navier-Stokes and scalar transport equations. Such systems, found in applications like turbidity currents and thermal convection, feature strong nonlinear coupling and multiscale behavior that make high-fidelity simulations computationally expensive. To address this, the chapter surveys state-of-the-art SciML methods for building efficient surrogate models, including linear reduced-order techniques based on Singular Value Decomposition (such as Dynamic Mode Decomposition) and nonlinear neural network approaches like Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and $β$-Variational Autoencoders ($β$-VAEs). It first covers the authors' work combining these models with High Performance Computing strategies, including Adaptive Mesh Refinement/Coarsening (AMR/C) and scientific floating-point data compression. It then presents two new contributions: surrogate modeling of turbidity currents via PINNs, and the extraction of disentangled nonlinear modes from thermal flows using $β$-VAEs. Governing equations and representative benchmarks, including lock-exchange flows and Rayleigh-Bénard convection, illustrate these methodologies. The chapter is intentionally long, covering both the mathematical and physical foundations of coupled fluid flow and the computational aspects of state-of-the-art modeling. Overall, it demonstrates how SciML enables fast, accurate approximations of complex coupled systems within the specific data regimes and modeling assumptions considered, while substantially reducing computational cost relative to full-order simulations. Broader capabilities such as real-time prediction and uncertainty quantification remain active research directions whose feasibility depends strongly on the problem at hand.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Reviews SciML methods for coupled flow and transport, applicable to CFD of falling film heat transfer
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 综述了科学机器学习在耦合流动与输运中的方法，可用于降膜换热CFD
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 综述了科学机器学习在耦合流动与输运中的方法，可用于降膜换热CFD
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 综述了科学机器学习在耦合流动与输运中的方法，可用于降膜换热CFD
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reviews recent SciML methods, including PINNs and autoencoders, for building surrogate models of coupled fluid flow and heat/mass transport, enabling efficient simulation of systems like thermal convection relevant to falling film heat exchangers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文综述了利用科学机器学习（如PINN和变分自编码器）构建耦合流体流动与传热传质代理模型的最新进展。这些方法能够大幅降低高保真模拟的计算成本，特别适用于热对流和浊流等多物理耦合问题，为降膜换热器CFD模拟提供了高效替代方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文综述了利用科学机器学习（如PINN和变分自编码器）构建耦合流体流动与传热传质代理模型的最新进展。这些方法能够大幅降低高保真模拟的计算成本，特别适用于热对流和浊流等多物理耦合问题，为降膜换热器CFD模拟提供了高效替代方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找与传热管垂直降膜换热、CFD模拟相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: heat transfer in falling film; falling film on vertical tube; two-phase flow simulation; liquid film thickness measurement; multiphase flow CFD; heat and mass transfer in falling film; CFD modeling of falling film heat transfer in vertical tubes; Numerical simulation of heat transfer in vertical falling film heat exchangers; Heat transfer characteristics of falling film over vertical tubes using computational fluid dynamics; Falling film evaporation and heat transfer in vertical tubes CFD.
